name: Best Bitter
description: The British pub classic. Malty, balanced, sessionable. Heritage Maris Otter and English noble hops over a fruity ale yeast. Cask-conditioned in spirit, but bottle works fine for homebrewers.

packaging._note: Lower carbonation (1.8 vols) and warmer serving temp (12°C) is traditional. If kegging, target 1.8-2.0 vols.

expected_profile.overall_summary: Bready, biscuity, gently fruity. Maris Otter malt does the heavy lifting; EKG and Fuggle add an earthy, slightly floral hop layer; S-04's stone-fruit esters tie it together. A pint of England.
expected_profile.finish: Dry-leaning balanced finish with lingering biscuity malt and a soft hop note

tips: Maris Otter is the soul of this style. Don't substitute pale 2-row.
Serve warmer than you think — 12°C, not lager-cold. Cold suppresses the malt character.
Lower carbonation is correct for the style. If pouring from a keg, dial it back to ~1.8 vols.
S-04 floccs hard — let it sit for 4 days at room temp after primary before crashing for proper clarity.
If you can keg, conditioning at 12°C for a week before serving is worth the wait.
common_mistakes: Serving too cold (loses the malt character)
Substituting US 2-row for Maris Otter (the malt IS the recipe here)
Using American hops (changes the style entirely — that's an APA, not a Bitter)
